title: ThonBoka
content:
{{Top|canon=StarCave Nebula}} {{Nebula |image=[[File:Oswaft_vs_Empire.jpg]] |name=ThonBoka |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the sector in both [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] as defined by ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |sector=[[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} |coordinates=U-8 |xyz= |distance= |system= |suns= |planets= |stations= |asteroids= |comets= |routes= |other= |formed= |size= |distinctions= |species=[[Oswaft]]''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' |otherspecies= |language= |government= |population= |interest= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= |battles1=[[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]] conflicts |b1=[[Battle of ThonBoka|ThonBoka]] |battles2= |b2= |battles3= |b3= |battles4= |b4= |battles5= |b5= |battles6= |b6= }} {{Quote|We are the ThonBoka; the ThonBoka is the Oswaft.|[[Senwannus'gourkahipaff]]|}} The '''ThonBoka''', also known as the '''ThonBoka Nebula''', was a sack-shaped [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] full of gases, organic molecules, carapace-creatures and interstellar [[Plankton/Legends|plankton]]. Its name translated into [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] as "'''StarCave Nebula'''." It was home to the sentient [[Oswaft]] [[Species/Legends|species]], a vacuum-dwelling race that had a unique natural ability to make [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps. The isolated location of the ThonBoka ensured the Oswaft lived in near-total obscurity until the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] learned of their existence and came to see the pacifistic species as a threat. The Empire [[Battle of ThonBoka|blockaded]] the ThonBoka in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], attempting to starve the Oswaft into extinction, although it was eventually forced to retreat. ==Description== {{Quote|But Master, there aren't any planets here to wreck, thank the Core."
"Thank three little blue suns out there that went kablooie for that.|[[Vuffi Raa]] and Lando Calrissian|}} The ThonBoka [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]]—which translated into [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic]] as the "StarCave"—was a sack-shaped cloud of dust and gas about a dozen [[Light-year/Legends|light-years]] across hidden within the remote [[Open Sea/Legends|Open Sea]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] region. Rife with preorganic molecules and amino acids, the ThonBoka was occupied by a vacuum-dwelling [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] called the [[Oswaft]], along with thousands of species of native carapace-creatures and interstellar [[Plankton/Legends|plankton]] that floated along the ThonBoka's currents. The ThonBoka was an open system where chemicals and nutrients freely passed through on the interstellar tide, which was crucial to the Oswaft's survival. From within, the gas and dust of the ThonBoka appeared to shut out the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], resembling the interior of an enormous cave. That cave-like impression was reinforced by the nebula's billows and folds that resembled geologic flows and sheets of [[Limestone/Legends|limestone]]. The nebula could only be entered from one side,[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] and that barrier was especially impermeable for the Oswaft, who traveled longer distances primarily through their natural ability to jump through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], as the gas and dust created a terminal amount of friction—it was said that Oswaft who tried to jump through the barrier would burst into flames and disappear. Three small, [[Planet/Legends|planetless]] [[Star/Legends|stars]], unusually located only about one or two light-years apart from one another, illuminated the ThonBoka from the region's center. ==History== {{Quote|That's quite a fleet for bottling up one undefended dust cloud. What do they think this is, the Clone Wars?|Lando Calrissian|}} During the era of the [[Great Galactic War]], the nebula was home to a fairly notorious group called the [[Skyraiders of ThonBoka]].[[The Old Republic — The Lost Suns 1|''The Old Republic — The Lost Suns'' 1]] However, by the time of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]] several millennia later, the ThonBoka had fallen into obscurity, only inhabited by the native Oswaft, who were almost completely unknown by the wider galaxy. Th Oswaft's existence was known in the Centrality for centuries, but this knowledge was kept from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and from the wider galaxy.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' It was only in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], when a young, uncharacteristically curious Oswaft named [[Lehesu]] decided to explore his home's surroundings, that anyone beyond the Centrality became aware of the Oswaft's existence. Lehesu's travels outside his people's ancestral territory aroused the suspicion and hostility of the Empire, which saw the peaceful Oswaft as a potential threat due to their numbers, massive size, and unique ability to naturally jump through hyperspace. The Empire arrived in force and laid siege to the ThonBoka, hoping to starve the Oswaft out by preventing the free flow of nourishment that sustained them. The Oswaft were pacifistic to their core, and some were willing to allow the Empire to wipe their species out rather than fight back. However, the deaths of Elder [[Bhoggihalysahonues]] and an Oswaft delegation that attempted to negotiate with the invading [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]—and the entreaties of [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], who had befriended Lehesu during his travels beyond the ThonBoka—rallied the Oswaft to struggle instead. The situation escalated into a bloody [[Battle of ThonBoka|battle]] between the Imperial force, commanded by [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]], and the Oswaft, which ended with Gepta's [[Death/Legends|death]] in single combat with Calrissian and with the arrival of the [[Silentium]], a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] culture that had arrived to safeguard one of their number, Calrissian's companion [[Vuffi Raa]]. The Silentium's power appeared so overwhelming that it cowed the Imperials into retreating quietly, once again leaving the ThonBoka safe for the Oswaft. ==Locations== As the native Oswaft soared freely on the cosmic winds within the ThonBoka, the nebula only had a single artificial structure, the only one the Oswaft ever constructed in their history: the [[Cave of Elders]]. Constructed as an exacting replica of the ThonBoka itself, the Cave of Elders—a rotating sphere which was entirely made of precious gems—{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}} was located in the triangular plane between the three stars in the interior of the nebula. The locale served as an important meeting place for the Oswaft, and was the location where their society's three Elders governed their people. ==Inhabitants== The ThonBoka was home to a native sentient species known as the Oswaft, who viewed the isolated region as their refuge, and as a place of peace and plenty. The Oswaft believed themselves bound to the nebula, and could not conceive of life outside its borders—they lived in symbiosis with the ThonBoka, feeding off the wildlife and debris that floated along its currents, and did not learn anything of life beyond the nebula until 3 BBY. Although the ThonBoka was large and billions of Oswaft called it home, the Oswaft maintained a homogeneous culture, and their ability to communicate [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] over vast distances prevented separate languages from ever forming. ==Behind the scenes== The ThonBoka appeared as the primary setting of ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'', written by [[L. Neil Smith]] and released in [[1983]]. ==Appearances== *[[The Old Republic — The Lost Suns 1|''The Old Republic — The Lost Suns'' 1]] {{Mo}} *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Starcave of ThonBoka]]'' {{1st}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/fiction/f20080522/index.html|text="Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array"|int=Lando Calrissian: Idiot's Array|newhost=Suvudu}} {{Mo}} *[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] {{Ab|Millennium Falcon (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the ThonBoka}} *{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] *{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} *''[[Millennium Falcon Owner's Workshop Manual]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ===Non-canon sources=== *{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}} {{Un}} {{ID|ThonBoka Nebula}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Thon Boka |es=ThonBoka |nl=ThonBoka }} [[Category:Centrality locations]] [[Category:Nebulae in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Nebulae in the Slice]]
